description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the alpine Docker image with two stages: test and deploy. In the test stage add a run pylint job that installs apk packages listed in apk-requirements.txt via `apk --no-cache add $(cat apk-requirements.txt)`, installs Python dependencies with `pip install -r requirements.txt`, installs the package in editable mode with `pip install -e .`, and runs `python tests/run_pylint.py`. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs the same apk install, then `pip install -r requirements.txt`, writes the tag to .version with `echo $CI_COMMIT_TAG > .version`, installs the package editable, builds HTML docs with `make html -C docs`, moves the output to a public directory with `mv docs/build/html/ public/`, and publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact, only on tag pipelines. Also in the deploy stage add a publish to pypi job that installs apk packages, installs twine via `pip install twine`, writes the tag to .version, builds a source distribution with `python setup.py sdist`, uploads it with `twine upload dist/*`, stores the dist/ directory as an artifact, and runs only on tags.
